macro_segments.title: The Debate on How Much the Inner Resident Must Pay When Surrounded by Walls

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna's Position: Payment Based on Full Cost of Actual Wall
Chiya bar Rav's Position: Payment Based on Minimal Benefit (Cheap Wood Cost)
Principle of Meshalem Mah She-Nehenis: Paying for Actual Benefit Not Actual Cost
Reconciling the Tanna Kamma and Rabbi Yose Through Rav Huna's Framework
Alternative: Payment Based on Cheap Wood Fence Cost (Chiya bar Rav's Approach)
Third Alternative: Payment Based on Watchman's Wages (Minimal Benefit)

macro_segments.title: The Debate on Which Walls Must Be Paid For: Full Circumference or Only the Fourth Wall

micro_segments.title: Tanna Kamma Position: Payment Only for Fourth Wall (Status Quo Argument)
Rabbi Yose Position: Payment for All Four Walls (Full Benefit)
Rashi's Explanation: Time and Status Quo
Tosafot's Explanation: Implicit Preference for Fourth Wall

macro_segments.title: Who Built the Fourth Wall: Makif (Outer) vs. Nikaf (Inner)

micro_segments.title: First Reading: Debate Based on Which Walls to Pay For (Makif/Nikaf)
Second Reading: Debate Based on Who Actually Builds the Fourth Wall
Tanna Kamma: Outer Guy Builds Fourth Wall, Inner Must Pay Portion
Rabbi Yose: Only Inner Guy Building Wall Obligates Full Payment

macro_segments.title: The Case of Ravina and Runya: Practical Application of Payment Obligations

micro_segments.title: Setup: Ravina Surrounds Runya's Field with Walls from Four Sides
Ravina Demands Full Stone Wall Payment; Runya Refuses
Ravina's Compromise Offers: Stone Wall, Wood Fence, Then Watchman's Wages
Ravina Tests Runya's Preferences by Threatening to Steal Dates
Ravina Argues Runya Needs Protection Even from Goats (Demonstrating Benefit)
Runya Counters: I Would Only Hire a Watchman, Not Build a Wall
Case Brought Before Rava: Directive to Compromise or Face Full Payment
Tosafot's Analysis: Is Rava's Threat Real or Merely Persuasive?

macro_segments.title: The Case of Bar Metzra (Right of First Refusal) and Arba Letzalla Arba Letzlalla

micro_segments.title: Runya Buys Adjacent Land; Ravina Invokes Bar Metzra to Seize Field
Legal Principle: Right of First Refusal Based on Ve'Asita Ha-Yashar Ve-Ha-Tov
Rashi's First Reading: Economic Compassion for the Poor
Rashi's Third Reading: Economy of Scale (Poor Tanner's Fixed Costs)

macro_segments.title: The Case of Bar Metzra (Right of First Refusal) and Arba Letzalla Arba Letzlalla (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tosafot's Challenge: How Can Ethical Obligation Override Legal Rights?

macro_segments.title: Fallen Wall and Shared Responsibility: Building and Payment Obligations

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Shared Wall Falls; Can Force Rebuilding Up to Four Cubits
Presumption of Joint Payment: Credited Unless Proven Otherwise
Building Higher Wall: Neighbor Building Parallel Wall Shows Agreement
Tosafot: Benefit Analysis When Wall Comes at No Cost to Builder

macro_segments.title: When Someone Claims They Already Paid: Fixed Loan Due Dates vs. Presumptions

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish: Not Believed to Claim Payment Before Explicit Due Date
Abaye and Rava: People Sometimes Do Pay Early; Believed to Claim Early Payment
Inferring from Shared Wall Mishna: Does It Support Early Payment Claims?
Gemara: Ongoing Construction Means Each Row Is Its Own Due Date
Second Mishna Case: Neighbor Obligated for Extra Height Only After Building
Gemara: Neighbor Skeptical of Obligation Until Beit Din Rules
Final Halacha: Reish Lakish's Position Adopted; Chazaka Against Early Payment
